stories.headline: Stephen Miller says welfare fraud could balance entire federal budget

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: 🚨 Stephen Miller says the scale of welfare fraud is SO MASSIVE that eliminating it alone could balance the ENTIRE federal budget "The amount that has been fleeced from us is in the HUNDREDS OF BILLIONS of dollars." "We could balance the federal budget if the only dollars that went out of the treasury went to individuals who were properly, lawfully, correctly eligible to receive them." This should infuriate EVERY taxpayer.

stories.headline: Marvel Rivals announces new Alchemax Headquarters map available May 28.
stories.body: ⚡ Power Unleashed: Welcome to Alchemax Headquarters! Step into the heart of corporate ambition and temporal chaos. Once the epicenter of Miguel O'Hara's groundbreaking genetic research, Alchemax HQ now stands as a high-stakes battlefield where the past, present, and future collide. From the sterile, high-tech intensity of the Main Gene Lab to the serene, deceptive beauty of the Babylon Garden, this corporate containment site for the raw, pulsing energy of Cyclops' optic blasts has been brought to the present by Moon Girl. Whether you're maneuvering through the sleek, nano-ceramic halls or taking in the view from the Infinity-Edge Pool overlooking the Manhattan skyline, remember: you're not just fighting for territory, you're fighting within the epicenter of a timeline-shattering rescue mission. Available from May 28th, 2026 UTC! The time has come to save the heroes and stop the sins of Alchemax!

stories.headline: Thousands of Cuban migrants deported from US face rights violations in Mexico, per Human Rights Watch report.
stories.body: Thousands of Cuban migrants deported from the United States have faced violations of their rights and are currently in “a legal limbo” in Mexico, where they have difficulties regularizing their situation and even obtaining medical care, according to a new report from Human Rights Watch.

stories.headline: Elon Musk posts a single skeptical emoji.
Joe Rogan reacts to Tom Segura's story about Uday Hussein
stories.body:
New: Joe Rogan visibly disturbed as Tom Segura brings up Uday Hussein who was the most evil man to ever live:

SEGURA: “I was reading about brutal dictators like Idi Amin, Mussolini, and Stalin. Uday Hussein was darker than all of them.”

ROGAN: “He would steal women that were getting married and feed them to his dogs.”

SEGURA: “He once killed a chef for oversalting his food.”

ROGAN: “200 people per year would die at his parties.”

SEGURA: “He would shoot people in the head at his parties if they didn’t laugh hard enough at his jokes.”

ROGAN: “Holy f*ck.”

stories.headline: Theo Von shares intense ketamine therapy experience on Joe Rogan podcast.
stories.body: Theo Von opened up about his ketamine therapy experience on Joe Rogan, and it was raw as hell. He did six IV sessions. During one, he started talking about his dad (who passed 20 years ago) and suddenly felt like he was actually with him again — real enough to cry and tell him he loved him. He also got to see his mom as a happy little kid. At times it felt like sliding off the edge of the universe with no ropes. Theo’s honesty about chasing relief from grief and anger through this was heavy, but powerful. This one really stuck with me. The way he describes those moments is so human and vulnerable. So many people are quietly struggling with grief, anger, and mental health. Hearing someone like Theo explore intense therapies with complete candor helps remove some of the stigma. Theo Von is one of the most real, funny, and courageous voices out there right now. Respect.
